scope: hook-interface-manifest — This manifest defines hook interfaces and the scryrs record invocation contract. It is not a tool catalog, MCP descriptor, agent-callable surface, or tool-output rewriter.

_anti_patterns._comment: The following are explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this manifest.
_anti_patterns.NOT_mcp_descriptor: This manifest does not define MCP methods, tools/capabilities, server descriptors, or agent-callable surfaces.
_anti_patterns.NOT_tool_catalog: This manifest does not register or catalog tools for agent consumption. Intercepted tool names are observational metadata describing what the hooks observe.
_anti_patterns.NOT_agent_callable: scryrs is not callable as an agent tool. Do not model it as a tool the agent can invoke.
_anti_patterns.NOT_tool_output_rewriter: This manifest does not describe tool output rewriting, augmentation, or modification.

hook.description: Harness integration entry point. Accepts a harness's native tool event, translates it via the scryrs-adapter-harness crate, and persists it through the same canonical store as `scryrs record`. Always exits 0 with empty stdout (fail-open) and never blocks the harness.

input.pi: raw tool_result/session_start event JSON via --file <PATH> (Pi exec opens stdin as /dev/null)

hook.failOpen: On any error (malformed input, unknown harness, translation failure, store error) the command exits 0, writes nothing to stdout, and appends a timestamped line to .scryrs/hooks/<harness>-warnings.log.

record.description: Low-level canonical-JSONL ingestion primitive beneath `scryrs hook`. Accepts validated TraceEvent JSONL on stdin.

alternateMode.description: Available alternate ingestion mode for reading trace events from a file instead of stdin.

outputContract.stderr: Rejection diagnostics — one JSON object per rejected non-empty line, with line, field, and reason fields.

exitCodes.1: One or more events rejected (partial acceptance), or I/O error.
exitCodes.2: Fatal usage or I/O error (mutually exclusive modes, unreadable file, store failure).

supported_harnesses.integration: Native `scryrs hook claude-code` command hook configured in .claude/settings.json under PreToolUse as {"type":"command","command":"scryrs hook claude-code"}. The PreToolUse event JSON is piped on stdin. No JavaScript, no node runtime, and no reference hook file.
supported_harnesses._interceptedToolsNote: Observational metadata — these are documented PascalCase Claude Code tool names observed by the hook, not a callable surface. debugOnlyInterceptedTools are only captured when SCRYRS_DEBUG is set to a non-empty value.
supported_harnesses.limitations: PreToolUse-only hook. Fires pre-execution, so every event carries outcome=Success. Fail-open is exit 0 with empty stdout. No SessionStart, SessionEnd, PostToolUse, or notification-level events are captured.

supported_harnesses.integration: Thin in-process extension (Pi loads a module rather than spawning a subprocess hook). The shim resolves session_id from Pi's SessionManager, serializes the raw tool_result/session_start event to a temp file, and delegates translation to `scryrs hook pi --file <tmp>`. It contains no tool→event mapping.
supported_harnesses._interceptedToolsNote: Observational metadata — these are lowercase Pi tool names observed by the shim, not a callable surface. debugOnlyInterceptedTools are only captured when SCRYRS_DEBUG is set to a non-empty value.
supported_harnesses.limitations: SessionEnd is deferred. Pi's session_shutdown event fires after the hook runtime has already torn down, so SessionEnd events cannot be captured at this time.
